sglang.title: [FixBug] [Diffusion] Fix Qwen-Image-Edit Lightning LoRA alpha/rank scaling (read per-layer *.alpha)
sglang.author: qichu-yun
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Some LoRA checkpoints (e.g., Lightning distill) encode per-layer scaling via an additional ".alpha" entry. Without reading this value, the runtime may apply an incorrect default scaling (effectively assuming α=r), leading to mismatched LoRA strength and degraded/incorrect outputs compared

sglang.title: [BugFix]support torch.compile use flash attention v4
sglang.author: ahb13
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When using sglang to generate video on B200, I find that after enabling the --enable-torch-compile option, an error occurs at the end of the run.
The command to  generate  video is:
SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_ENABLED=true \
SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_WARMUP=4 \
SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_MC=8 \
SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_RDT=0.

sglang.author: ByronHsu

Motivation

When we use logprob + streaming, tokenizer manager could hang because for every chunk, it re-process (detokenize_logprob_tokens) every token from the beginning. Say if we have 1000 tokens, it process 1, 2, 3, ..., 1000 tokens in each chunk. For each token (in the topk case), it creates a

sglang.title: feat: add --admin-api-key for finer-grained endpoint auth
sglang.author: alphabetc1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Previously, there was only a single “global API key” mechanism: if server_args.api_key was set, auth was enabled and applied almost uniformly to all routes (except /health*, /metrics*, and OPTIONS). Anyone with that one api_key could call sensitive management endpoints like /update_weight

vllm.title: [Kernel][Performance] Enable smaller Scaling Factor tiling for NVFP4 small-batch decoding
vllm.author: LopezCastroRoberto
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR adds an opt-in NVFP4 backend variant that uses smaller scaling-factor tiling (8x4 SF layout). The change targets small-concurrency decode workloads and delivers ~25–35% higher output token throughput compared to the current best NVFP4 backend at small batch sizes.

Purpose
This PR adds support for Molmo2 to vLLM.
Molmo2 is a family of open vision-language models developed by the Allen Institute for AI (Ai2) that support image, video and multi-image understanding and grounding.
This change:

Introduces a new Molmo2 model implementation for vLLM

vllm.title: [Model] Re-implement Qwen3Omni Audio Encoder

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
